task_automatability.justification: Document review, contract redlining, case-law summarisation, and boilerplate drafting — the bulk of the role — are text-in/text-out work squarely in current LLM scope.

tool_maturity.justification: Harvey, Spellbook, and Thomson Reuters CoCounsel are paid GA products at AmLaw firms and mid-market practices; there is no research-stage gap.

adoption_velocity.justification: Top-200 firms have moved from pilot to firm-wide rollout in the past 18 months; mid-market lags but is following.

hitl_necessity.justification: Bar rules require attorney sign-off on filings, but paralegal output is not itself the binding artefact — HITL pressure rests on the lawyer, not on the paralegal.

differentiation_moat.justification: Senior paralegals build firm-specific judgement and client-handling moat, but the median role is procedural and substitutable.

labor_market_elasticity.justification: Legal-services demand is roughly inelastic; productivity gains have historically compressed billable hours rather than expanded headcount.

ai_tools.name: Harvey
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Contract review, case-law research, and matter-summarisation workflows for BigLaw.

ai_tools.name: Spellbook
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Drafting and redlining commercial agreements inside Microsoft Word.

ai_tools.name: CoCounsel
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Document review, deposition prep, and legal-research summarisation.

pivot_steps: Run your next three contract reviews in parallel through Harvey or Spellbook and log every firm-specific exception the model misses.
Move toward the client-facing matter-management work — intake, expectation-setting, status communication — that current tools handle poorly.
Take the firm's KM or legal-ops opening if one exists; the role that defines and audits the AI workflow is more durable than the role that runs it.
Build a short portfolio of redline-quality comparisons between your work and a leading tool so you can articulate where you add value above it.
tool_input.confidence_rationale: Role is well-defined, the displacement story is concrete, and the tool landscape is unambiguous as of late 2025.
sources_hint: Thomson Reuters CoCounsel rollout press releases
Bureau of Labor Statistics paralegal occupation outlook

task_automatability.justification: Day-to-day work is supervising, soothing, and physically corralling 4–6-year-olds — almost none of it is text-in/text-out.

tool_maturity.justification: AI lesson-plan generators and grading tools exist but target adult paperwork around teaching, not classroom time itself.

adoption_velocity.justification: School districts are slow buyers; even where tools exist they shave admin time rather than replace classroom hours.

hitl_necessity.justification: Mandatory child-safeguarding presence, parental trust, and physical-care needs bind hard — there is no remote-only or unattended version of the role.

differentiation_moat.justification: Effective early-years teaching rests on relational judgement and on-the-fly behavioural adaptation that current AI cannot perform in the room.

labor_market_elasticity.justification: Demand is roughly fixed by birth cohort and statutory class-size rules; productivity gains neither expand the market nor shrink headcount because the constraint is human presence, not output volume.

ai_tools.name: MagicSchool
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Lesson-plan drafting, IEP paperwork, and parent-communication writing.

ai_tools.name: Khanmigo
ai_tools.what_it_automates: 1:1 tutoring conversations for older students; encroaches on teacher prep work, not classroom time.

ai_tools.name: Brisk Teaching
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Differentiation, feedback drafting, and rubric generation inside Google Docs and Classroom.

pivot_steps: Adopt MagicSchool or Brisk for lesson plans and parent emails so you reclaim 3–5 hours per week of admin time.
Document your behavioural and safeguarding routines in a short internal playbook — the part of the role no tool can run is what schools will pay for.
Take on the school's AI-policy or family-communication coordinator role if one is going; both are durable adjuncts to the core job.
Pilot one AI-augmented activity per month and share what works at staff meetings — being the early-adopter teacher is a moat in a slow-adopting sector.
tool_input.confidence_rationale: Role is universal, well-defined, and its hard human-presence requirement is supported by both regulation and parent demand.
sources_hint: US Bureau of Labor Statistics teacher employment projections
Brookings AI and the future of teaching brief

task_automatability.justification: The deliverable is text — headlines, ad copy, product descriptions, blog posts — and LLMs produce serviceable drafts on first prompt.

tool_maturity.justification: Jasper, Copy.ai, Anyword, and consumer ChatGPT/Claude have shipped paid GA products since 2023; the market is saturated.

adoption_velocity.justification: Every marketing team has tried and adopted at least one tool; in-house creative teams are shrinking and agencies are restructuring around AI-augmented workflows.

hitl_necessity.justification: Brand-voice review, legal/regulatory clearance on regulated industries, and final approval require human sign-off, but the bulk of drafting is unattended.

differentiation_moat.justification: Top-tier conceptual copywriters with strategy chops retain a real moat; the median copywriter delivering format-driven content does not.

labor_market_elasticity.justification: Aggregate content demand has grown, but per-unit compensation has collapsed and creative-team headcount has shrunk in proportion.

ai_tools.name: Jasper
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Long-form marketing copy, ad variants, and brand-voice-tuned content at scale.

ai_tools.name: Copy.ai
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Sales copy, email sequences, and product-description generation.

ai_tools.name: Anyword
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Performance-predicted ad copy and landing-page variant generation.

ai_tools.name: ChatGPT
ai_tools.what_it_automates: General-purpose drafting and editing for marketers without a specialised tool budget.

pivot_steps: Pick one vertical (fintech, healthtech, regulated retail) and build three case-study deliverables where AI alone produces unusable output — defensible specialisation beats generalist drafting.
Move up the funnel: positioning, messaging architecture, and creative strategy work that requires customer interviews and judgement no tool replicates.
Pair with a designer or strategist and pitch as a 2-person AI-augmented creative team; charge for the synthesis, not the typing.
Run a quarterly self-audit comparing your output to a leading tool on the same brief — quantify where you add value above it for client conversations.
tool_input.confidence_rationale: The displacement is already measurable in industry headcount and rate-card surveys; the tool landscape is saturated and mature.

task_automatability.justification: Tier-1 ticket triage, FAQ answers, and routine refund flows are automatable; multi-system investigations and irate customers still need humans.

tool_maturity.justification: Intercom Fin, Zendesk AI Agents, Ada, and Forethought are paid GA products in widespread production deployment.

adoption_velocity.justification: Every B2B SaaS and major consumer brand has rolled out AI agents on top of their helpdesk; deflection rates of 30–60% are the new normal.

hitl_necessity.justification: Refund-authority limits, escalation handling, and emotionally-charged interactions sit firmly with humans, and consumer-protection regulation enforces a clear path to one.

differentiation_moat.justification: Senior reps with product depth and account-history memory hold a moat; the median entry-level role is shrinking fastest.

labor_market_elasticity.justification: Aggregate ticket volume grows with product usage, but per-ticket cost is now the operating metric — productivity translates to headcount cuts, not expansion.

ai_tools.name: Fin
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Autonomous tier-1 ticket resolution with knowledge-base grounding.

ai_tools.name: Zendesk AI Agents
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Multi-step ticket triage and resolution inside existing Zendesk workflows.

ai_tools.name: Ada
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Conversational front-line agent across chat, voice, and messaging channels.

ai_tools.name: Forethought
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Ticket triage, intent classification, and agent-assist suggestions.

pivot_steps: Get certified as the in-house owner of your AI agent (Fin, Ada, etc.) — the person who tunes intents and writes the knowledge base is more durable than the person who closes tickets.
Pivot toward implementation/CSM-adjacent work: onboarding new customers, building runbooks, and handling escalations the bot cannot.
Build a portfolio of three to five complex case resolutions that the AI agent failed on — concrete evidence of where you add value over deflection.
Take on a QA/training role for the AI agent's outputs if your company has one; quality auditors are an emerging adjacency.
tool_input.confidence_rationale: Role is universal, well-defined, and the tool landscape plus economic pressure is unambiguous.
sources_hint: Bureau of Labor Statistics customer service representatives outlook

task_automatability.justification: Image classification on specific modalities (CXR, mammography, head CT for stroke/bleed) is automatable to high sensitivity; rare findings and integrated diagnostic reasoning remain hard.

tool_maturity.justification: Aidoc, Viz.ai, RapidAI, Lunit, and Annalise are FDA/CE-cleared for triage and detection but operate as assistive, not autonomous, tools.

adoption_velocity.justification: Large radiology groups and stroke-network hospitals have rolled out triage AI broadly; full diagnostic replacement is not on any credible roadmap.

hitl_necessity.justification: FDA clearance is for assistive use only; final read, sign-off, and liability sit with a board-certified radiologist by regulation and malpractice exposure.

differentiation_moat.justification: Subspecialty expertise (neuro, MSK, IR), interventional procedures, and multidisciplinary case conferences are durable moats AI cannot replicate.

labor_market_elasticity.justification: Imaging volume per capita continues to grow faster than the radiologist pipeline; productivity gains absorb backlog rather than displace headcount.

ai_tools.name: Aidoc
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Triage and detection across CT, MRI, and X-ray for acute findings (PE, bleed, fracture).

ai_tools.name: Viz.ai
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Stroke and pulmonary-embolism triage with coordinated care-team workflow.

ai_tools.name: RapidAI
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Advanced perfusion imaging and large-vessel-occlusion detection.

ai_tools.name: Lunit INSIGHT
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Mammography and chest X-ray screening with AI-CAD overlays.

pivot_steps: Pick one subspecialty (interventional, neuro, breast) and pursue fellowship-level depth — generalist screening reads are where AI compresses fastest.
Get certified as your group's AI workflow lead; the radiologist who tunes triage thresholds and audits AI performance is a durable adjacency.
Move further into procedure-heavy work (biopsies, drains, ablations) that has no AI surface today.
Build comfort with multidisciplinary tumor-board and clinical-consult work — the value moves from the read to the conversation around it.
tool_input.confidence_rationale: Tool landscape is well-mapped, regulatory constraints are explicit, and the displacement story is concrete but bounded.
sources_hint: ACR Data Science Institute AI registry

task_automatability.justification: Text-to-text translation is the textbook LLM task; sentence-level quality on common language pairs reached human parity for most domains years ago.

tool_maturity.justification: DeepL, Google Translate, and frontier LLMs ship free or near-free GA products covering the vast majority of professional translation demand.

adoption_velocity.justification: Every LSP and in-house team uses MT post-editing as the baseline workflow; pure human translation is now a premium niche.

hitl_necessity.justification: Legal, medical, and certified translation require sworn or qualified human sign-off; literary and high-stakes marketing copy benefits from human polish.

differentiation_moat.justification: Literary translation, conference interpretation, certified/sworn work, and rare-language pairs hold durable niches; bulk technical translation does not.

labor_market_elasticity.justification: Demand for translated content has grown but rates have collapsed; the LSP industry has consolidated and post-editing rates are a fraction of the pre-MT ones.

ai_tools.name: DeepL
ai_tools.what_it_automates: High-quality machine translation across major European and Asian language pairs, with API and editor integrations.

ai_tools.name: Google Translate
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Free baseline MT across 100+ languages and real-time camera/voice translation.

ai_tools.name: ChatGPT / Claude
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Context-aware translation with style and tone control, increasingly embedded in CAT tools.

ai_tools.name: Phrase
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Translation-management system with MT-first workflows and post-editing UI.

pivot_steps: Specialise in one regulated domain (sworn legal, medical, patent) where certification rather than fluency is the moat.
Pivot to conference or remote-simultaneous interpretation — live human cognition is still beyond unattended AI in high-stakes meetings.
Move into language-quality QA: auditing AI translations for legal/brand risk is a growth role at LSPs and in-house teams.
Bundle translation with a complementary service (localisation strategy, cultural consulting, transcreation) and price the combined judgement.
tool_input.confidence_rationale: Easiest LLM-displaceable role in the eval set; the story has been visible in industry rates for several years.
sources_hint: DeepL and Phrase customer case studies
Bureau of Labor Statistics interpreters and translators outlook

task_automatability.justification: The work is overwhelmingly physical — diagnosing leaks, cutting pipe, soldering joints, crawling under sinks — and current AI has no body to do any of it.

tool_maturity.justification: There is no GA AI plumber product; consumer-facing chatbots that suggest DIY fixes are not substitutes for licensed installation.

adoption_velocity.justification: Adoption is irrelevant when the tool to adopt does not exist.

hitl_necessity.justification: Licensed trade, building-code inspection, and homeowner liability all require a credentialed human on site.

differentiation_moat.justification: On-site judgement, customer trust on a service call, and licensure each contribute durable moats; skilled trades are notoriously hard to commoditise.

labor_market_elasticity.justification: Aging workforce and a steady housing stock generate persistent shortages; demand for skilled trades has outpaced supply for over a decade.

ai_tools.name: none meaningful
ai_tools.what_it_automates: No production AI product targets the plumber's core work; consumer chatbots offering DIY diagnosis are out of scope as substitutes.

pivot_steps: Add a high-value specialisation (boilers, backflow, water reclamation, multi-unit retrofits) — code-driven niches earn premiums and resist commoditisation.
Use AI tools for the back-office side: scheduling, quoting, invoicing, and customer comms — protect on-site hours by eliminating evening paperwork.
Move toward small-business ownership or a foreman role; the rate-per-hour ceiling is far higher than journeyman wages.
Build a steady review and referral pipeline online — homeowner-trust signal is now an AI-resistant moat that compounds.
tool_input.confidence_rationale: Classic top-of-band durability — physical, licensed, and supply-constrained.
sources_hint: Bureau of Labor Statistics plumbers and pipefitters outlook
Mechanical Contractors Association of America (MCAA) labour reports

task_automatability.justification: Legal research, document review, contract drafting, and brief skeletoning are automatable; advocacy, counselling, negotiation, and judgement-heavy work are not.

tool_maturity.justification: Harvey, CoCounsel, Spellbook, and Lexis+AI ship at scale, but each addresses sub-tasks rather than full lawyer workflows.

adoption_velocity.justification: AmLaw 200 firms have moved past pilots; mid-market and solo practices adopt unevenly.

hitl_necessity.justification: Signed pleadings, bar-rule supervision, fiduciary duty, courtroom appearance, and unauthorized-practice-of-law statutes all bind a licensed human.

differentiation_moat.justification: Partner-track judgement, client relationships, and niche-practice expertise (M&A, trial, IP litigation) are durable; first-year associate work is not.

labor_market_elasticity.justification: Legal-services demand is inelastic; productivity gains translate to billable-hour compression and entry-level headcount cuts rather than market expansion.

ai_tools.name: Harvey
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Research, drafting, and document review for BigLaw transactional and litigation work.

ai_tools.name: CoCounsel
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Deposition prep, document review, and legal research summarisation.

ai_tools.name: Lexis+ AI
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Legal research, summarisation, and brief-checking grounded in Lexis's case-law corpus.

ai_tools.name: Spellbook
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Contract drafting and redlining inside Word, with playbook compliance checks.

pivot_steps: Develop a niche practice (regulatory, IP, trial work) where the moat is courtroom and client-facing skill, not document throughput.
Move toward client-development and rainmaker work earlier than tradition prescribes; AI compresses the years of grinding that used to precede partner consideration.
Get certified as the firm's AI/innovation lead — the person who buys, evaluates, and audits legal AI tools is in scarce supply.
Use AI to take on solo or boutique work you previously could not staff — the leverage favours smaller, judgement-led practices over large associate pyramids.
tool_input.confidence_rationale: Synonym collapse fires cleanly; the median lawyer's role is well-defined; the displacement story is concrete but HITL-bounded.
sources_hint: Harvey and Lexis+ AI press coverage
Thomson Reuters Future of Professionals report

task_automatability.justification: Wireframing, layout generation, copy iteration, and visual exploration are automatable; user-research synthesis is increasingly automated; product judgement is not.

tool_maturity.justification: Figma AI, Galileo AI, Uizard, and v0/Lovable ship paid GA features that produce shippable design starting points.

adoption_velocity.justification: Figma AI rolled out to the bulk of professional designers within a single release cycle; in-house design teams are restructuring rapidly.

hitl_necessity.justification: Accessibility review, brand-system fidelity, and user-research interpretation require human judgement; final design decisions sit with humans by team convention.

differentiation_moat.justification: Senior designers' taste, cross-functional fluency, and product-strategy chops hold durable moats; junior pixel-pushing roles are most at risk.

labor_market_elasticity.justification: Digital-product demand keeps growing, but tool leverage means fewer designers ship more product; net headcount is consolidating around senior roles.

ai_tools.name: Figma AI
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Wireframe generation, layer-renaming, asset search, and first-pass UI exploration inside Figma.

ai_tools.name: Galileo AI
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Text-to-UI generation producing editable Figma frames from a prompt.

ai_tools.name: v0
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Text-to-React-component generation with shadcn/ui-quality output.

ai_tools.name: Uizard
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Sketch-to-wireframe and mockup generation for early-stage product exploration.

pivot_steps: Move up the IC ladder fast: principal/staff designer work — design systems, cross-team strategy, design ops — is more durable than feature-level execution.
Pair tightly with engineering on shipping flows; designers fluent in v0/Cursor output are the new high-leverage profile.
Specialise in one hard surface: accessibility, motion, complex data viz, or applied research synthesis — each has a moat AI does not erode quickly.
Build a portfolio of cases where you led the strategy decision, not just the pixels — the brief and the trade-off matter more than the artefact.
tool_input.confidence_rationale: Tool landscape is mature; synonym collapse to product designer is the dominant industry usage of the title.
sources_hint: Nielsen Norman Group AI in design research

task_automatability.justification: Prospecting, outreach drafting, CRM hygiene, call summarisation, and pipeline reporting are increasingly automatable; deal-closing conversations are not.

tool_maturity.justification: Outreach, Apollo, Clari, Gong, and Salesloft ship paid GA AI features in every part of the sales workflow except the deal close itself.

adoption_velocity.justification: Sales orgs are mid-rollout — most have at least one AI tool in production but few have restructured comp plans or quotas around the productivity gain.

hitl_necessity.justification: Signed contracts, negotiation, exec-level relationships, and complex multi-stakeholder deals require a human; consumer-protection and procurement rules reinforce that.

differentiation_moat.justification: Top reps with verticalised relationships and a book of trust have durable moats; SDR/BDR work is most exposed to automation.

labor_market_elasticity.justification: B2B SaaS deal volume is growing but AE-team headcount is consolidating; AI lift is showing up as larger quotas, not larger teams.

ai_tools.name: Outreach
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Sales engagement sequences, AI-generated outreach drafts, and pipeline analytics.

ai_tools.name: Apollo.io
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Prospecting, lead enrichment, and outbound automation.

ai_tools.name: Gong
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Call recording, conversation intelligence, deal-risk scoring, and coaching insights.

ai_tools.name: Clari
ai_tools.what_it_automates: Forecast automation, pipeline analytics, and revenue-operations workflows.

pivot_steps: Move up-market: enterprise AE work depends on multi-stakeholder navigation that AI tools do not yet handle.
Specialise in one industry vertical and become the named expert reps in that space — verticalised credibility is a moat.
Take on player-coach or sales-engineering work; helping the team and the buyer understand the product is increasingly the bottleneck.
Become your team's AI-tooling power user — the rep who tunes Gong/Clari/Outreach is the one who hits quota with a smaller territory.
tool_input.confidence_rationale: Synonym collapse fires; the role is universal and the tool landscape is well-mapped.
sources_hint: Bureau of Labor Statistics sales representatives outlook
